title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy skin
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy skin are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, created by soaking plant material in a solvent such as alcohol or vinegar. These tinctures can provide relief for itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory, antihistamine, and soothing properties. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with itchy skin include chamomile, calendula, and nettle. Chamomile tincture, with its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, can reduce skin inflammation and histamine-related itching. Calendula tincture, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, can soothe irritated skin and promote healing. Nettle tincture, known for its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects, can alleviate itching caused by skin allergies or inflammation. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into their skincare routine, individuals can find relief from itchy skin, enhancing their overall quality of life and comfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and anti-pruritic properties that reduce inflammation and alleviate itching. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, work together to soothe irritated skin and promote healing. Yarrow tincture also has astringent properties that help to tighten and tone the skin, reducing the likelihood of further irritation. Its anti-microbial and anti-oxidant properties further protect the skin from infection and damage, providing additional relief from itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property reduces redness and swelling associated with itchy skin, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property helps dry up oozing skin lesions and reduces itching, due to the tannins present in the plant.
Antipruritic: Yarrow tincture's antipruritic property directly addresses itchiness by reducing histamine production, which is triggered by allergens and irritants.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic property helps alleviate the pain associated with itchy skin, thanks to its active constituents like volatile oils and alkaloids.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial property prevents skin infections that can exacerbate itching, which is attributed to its active constituents like lactones, flavonoids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, yarrow flowers help soothe itchy skin and reduce redness.
Leaves: Containing tannins and volatile oils, yarrow leaves can help alleviate itching and provide astringent properties.
Stems: With similar properties to leaves, yarrow stems can help treat itchy skin while providing extra structural support for tincture preparation.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds can help moisturize and soothe itchy skin, promoting overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves stems flowers at dawn or evening when essential oils most concentrated.
Chop or grind 200g of plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 400ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar filling it about 90%.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can soothe and heal irritated skin. Burdock's active compounds, such as arctigen and lignans, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can cause skin irritations. Additionally, burdock has demulcent properties, which means it can form a protective layer on the skin, locking in moisture and providing relief from dryness and itchiness. Burdock tincture can be used topically or taken internally to treat various skin conditions, including eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. By promoting skin health and reducing inflammation, burdock tincture can help alleviate the discomfort and itchiness associated with skin irritations.
remedy_properties: Anti-pruritic: Burdock tincture can help alleviate itchy skin by reducing the urge to scratch, thanks to its active constituents like tannins and essential fatty acids.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of burdock tincture help soothe itchy skin by reducing redness and swelling, due to the presence of arctiin and lignans.
Antimicrobial: Burdock tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can cause itchy skin, as a result of its antimicrobial properties from compounds like phenolic acids and quinones.
Demulcent: Burdock tincture creates a protective barrier on the skin, which helps retain moisture and reduce irritation, due to its mucilaginous constituents such as inulin and polysaccharides.

Diuretic: Burdock tincture promotes the elimination of toxins from the body, which can contribute to itchy skin, by stimulating the kidneys and increasing urine production, thanks to its diuretic active constituents like flavonoids and glycosides.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them ideal for soothing itchy skin and treating skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis.
Leaves: Burdock leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat skin irritations, as they possess anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties that can help alleviate itchiness.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are rich in essential fatty acids and contain anti-inflammatory properties, making them beneficial for reducing skin inflammation and relieving itchy skin.

Flowers: Burdock flowers have been used in herbal remedies for treating skin issues, as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe itchy skin and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctium lappa roots dig freshly from the wild or cultivated source.
Cleanse the harvested roots thoroughly rinse with cold water and remove any debris.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces air dry for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steep the dried root pieces in high-proof grain alcohol 40% v/v at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and anti-pruritic properties that reduce inflammation and alleviate itching. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, promote skin healing and soothe irritation. Pot marigold tincture can be applied directly to the affected area or taken orally, providing quick relief for itchy skin. Its natural anti-bacterial and anti-fungal properties also help prevent skin infections that can exacerbate itching. Overall, pot marigold tincture is a gentle and effective herbal remedy for treating itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy skin because it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with skin irritation. This property is attributed to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antipruritic: Pot marigold tincture has an antipruritic effect, which means it helps alleviate itching. This property is crucial for treating itchy skin, as it directly addresses the primary symptom. The antipruritic effect is attributed to the plant's triterpene content.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture has antimicrobial properties, which are important for itchy skin because they help prevent and treat skin infections that can exacerbate itchiness. The antimicrobial activity is attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids, essential oils, and alkaloids.

Emollient: Pot marigold tincture has emollient properties, which help soothe and moisturize the skin. This is important for itchy skin because dryness can exacerbate itchiness and lead to further irritation. The emollient effect is attributed to the plant's high content of fatty acids and waxes.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for itchy skin because it can help speed up the recovery process and alleviate discomfort. This property is attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which promote tissue regeneration and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, making them effective in soothing itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing skin irritation and itchiness.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils with anti-pruritic (anti-itch) properties, making them a popular choice for treating itchy skin.
Roots: Although less commonly used, the roots of the pot marigold plant contain antimicrobial compounds, which can help prevent skin infections and alleviate itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly for two days.
Combine dried calendula officinalis flowers with vodka in a clean glass jar to achieve a ratio of 1:5.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking it daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and filtering if necessary.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place after four months allowing flavors and properties to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can soothe and heal irritated skin. The active compounds in chamomile, such as chamazulene and bisabolol, have a calming effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation. Chamazulene also has antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of itchy skin. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, providing instant relief to itchiness. When applied topically, chamomile tincture can help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation, providing quick and effective relief for itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: German chamomile tincture helps reduce itchy skin by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, such as alpha-bisabolol and chamazulene.
Anti-pruritic property: It helps alleviate itchy skin by directly countering the sensation of itch, due to the presence of anti-pruritic compounds like matricin and apigenin.

Anti-microbial property: German chamomile tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms, preventing skin infections that can cause itchiness, due to its active constituents, such as azulene and flavonoids.

Anti-oxidant property: It promotes skin health and soothes itchy skin by neutralizing free radicals and promoting tissue regeneration, owing to its antioxidant components, including terpenoids and phenolic compounds.
Analgesic property: German chamomile tincture helps relieve itchy skin by numbing the affected area, thanks to its active constituents, such as matricin and bisabolol.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in azulene, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them ideal for treating itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from itchiness.
Seeds: The seeds are a great source of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them beneficial for treating itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried chamomilla flowers in the morning when they are fully open.
Measure out one part dried or three parts fresh chamomilla flowers by weight.
Combine the measured herb with 40% vodka or grain alcohol and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after two hours.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least six weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that alleviate irritation and reduce redness. Chicory is rich in antioxidants and vitamins, which help to repair damaged skin and promote the growth of new cells. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory agents, such as sesquiterpene lactones, work to decrease inflammation, relieving itchiness and discomfort. Chicory tincture's cooling effect helps to soothe and calm itchy skin, providing quick and effective relief. Its antibacterial properties also help to protect the skin from infection, promoting overall skin health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chicory tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy skin as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with skin irritations. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids.

Antipruritic: Chicory tincture has an antipruritic effect, which means it helps relieve itching. This property is crucial for itchy skin because it directly targets the primary symptom, providing relief and comfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones and bitter glycosides.

Demulcent: Chicory tincture has demulcent properties, forming a soothing film over the skin, which helps protect and relieve irritated skin. This property is important for itchy skin as it helps create a barrier between the skin and external irritants, reducing itchiness and promoting healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include mucilage and polysaccharides.

Astringent: Chicory tincture has astringent properties, which help tighten and tone the skin. This property is important for itchy skin as it can help reduce redness and inflammation, while also promoting skin repair and regeneration. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and phenolic acids.

Analgesic: Chicory tincture has analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort. This property is important for itchy skin as it can help reduce the intensity and frequency of itching, allowing the skin to heal. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots contain anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, making them ideal for soothing itchy skin.
Leaves: Chicory leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to alleviate skin irritation and reduce itching.
Flowers: Chicory flowers contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation and provide relief for itchy skin.
Seeds: Chicory seeds are a great source of mucilage, which can help to soothe and protect itchy skin by forming a protective barrier.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried chicorium intybus roots and clean them thoroughly of dirt debris and excess material.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and mix with 60% vodka and 40% distilled water in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks allowing the mixture to steep and extract its active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Transfer the resulting tincture into small amber glass bottles seal tightly label and store in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains high levels of echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compound. This compound works to soothe and reduce inflammation in the skin, providing relief from itchiness. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to boost the immune system and promote wound healing, which can help to prevent further skin irritation and infections. The tincture form allows for easy absorption and quick relief, making it a convenient and effective option for treating itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy skin because inflammation can exacerbate skin irritation and itching. This property is attributed to the presence of eudespermic acid and other anti-inflammatory compounds.

Antipruritic: The tincture has antipruritic properties, which help alleviate itching. This is essential for itchy skin as it directly addresses the main symptom. The antipruritic effect is primarily due to the presence of cichoric acid and other active constituents.

Antimicrobial: Eastern purple coneflower tincture exhibits antimicrobial properties, which are important for itchy skin because bacterial or fungal infections can cause or worsen skin itching. The antimicrobial activity is attributed to the presence of essential oils and alkaloids in the tincture.

Analgesic: The tincture has analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain or discomfort associated with itchy skin. This is important because itchy skin can often be painful or uncomfortable, and reducing pain can improve overall skin health. The analgesic effect is primarily due to the presence of alkamides and other active constituents.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture has antioxidant properties, which are important for itchy skin because antioxidants help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Antioxidants found in the tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect skin cells from oxidative stress and support skin health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective for soothing itchy skin.
Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have been used in tinctures to help relieve skin irritation and itchiness.
Roots: Packed with medicinal compounds, including echinacea, which has been shown to help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy skin.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and active compounds that can help moisturize and soothe itchy, dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers, leaves, and stems from your garden or local market, ensuring sufficient quantity for the desired volume of tincture.
Clean and dry the plant material by gently shaking off excess dirt, then spreading it out in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth to air-dry for 24-48 hours.
Chop the dried echinacea plant material into small pieces using scissors or a food processor, ensuring uniform size to facilitate efficient extraction.
Combine one part chopped echinacea with three parts high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, stirring gently to ensure thorough coating of plant material for 2-6 weeks at room temperature.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and storing the resulting liquid in dark-tinted glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, allowing it to mature for an additional 4-6 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation. Horsetail is rich in silica, which can help to strengthen and repair the skin's connective tissues, promoting skin health and reducing itchiness. Additionally, horsetail has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that can help to combat any potential infections that may be contributing to itchy skin. When applied topically, horsetail tincture can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy skin as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with skin irritation. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Astringent: Horsetail tincture has an astringent effect, helping to tighten and dry out the skin. This is crucial for itchy skin because it can help shrink dilated blood vessels and reduce secretions, thereby alleviating itchiness. The astringency is primarily due to the tannins present in horsetail.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms, making it important for itchy skin as it helps prevent infections that can exacerbate skin irritation. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the silica and other phenolic compounds found in horsetail.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tincture neutralizes free radicals, which helps protect the skin from oxidative damage. This is significant for itchy skin as it aids in maintaining skin health and reducing inflammation. The antioxidant effects are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Demulcent: Horsetail tincture forms a soothing film over the skin, protecting it from external irritants. This is vital for itchy skin because it provides a barrier that reduces direct contact with irritants, thereby alleviating itchiness. The demulcent properties are attributed to the mucilage content in horsetail.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them ideal for treating itchy skin.
Stems: Horsetail stems are rich in silica, which can help reduce inflammation and itching, and promote skin healing.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-itch and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for soothing itchy skin.
Roots: Although not commonly used, horsetail roots can also be used to make medicinal tinctures for itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense leaves and stems, ensuring cleanliness and minimal contamination.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine one part of the chopped horsetail with two parts of a high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can soothe and heal irritated skin. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole, help reduce inflammation and redness, providing relief from itching. Additionally, fennel's antimicrobial properties can help prevent skin infections that may cause itchiness. Furthermore, fennel has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with itchy skin. Overall, fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and cooling properties make it an effective natural remedy for itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy skin as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with skin irritations. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Antipruritic: Fennel tincture has an antipruritic effect, which means it helps alleviate itching, making it an ideal remedy for itchy skin. The antipruritic property can be attributed to the anethole and flavonoids found in fennel.

Antibacterial: Fennel tincture has antibacterial properties that help prevent and treat skin infections, contributing to overall skin health and reducing the likelihood of itchy skin issues. The antibacterial properties are primarily due to the presence of trans-anethole and fenchone.

Antioxidant: Fennel tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from free radical damage, promoting skin health and reducing itchiness caused by oxidative stress. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the high levels of flavonoids and phenolic compounds in fennel.

Demulcent: Fennel tincture has a soothing, demulcent effect on the skin, creating a protective barrier and reducing irritation. This property is important for itchy skin as it helps to alleviate discomfort and promote healing. The demulcent effect is primarily due to the mucilage content in fennel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to have soothing effects on irritated skin, making them ideal for tinctures.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them useful for relieving itchy skin.
Leaves: Fennel leaves have a high concentration of essential oils, which have been shown to have a calming effect on itchy skin.
Rhizomes: Fennel rhizomes are used in medicinal tinctures for itchy skin due to their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems, selecting fresh and free of contaminants.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into small pieces, removing any excess moisture.
Combine one part chopped fennel with one part vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, ensuring proper mixing.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, allowing the plant's bioactive compounds to extract into the solvent.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate skin irritation and reduce redness. The herb's active compounds, such as secoiridoid glycosides and xanthones, have been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, helping to relieve itching and discomfort. Additionally, yellow gentian tincture may help to improve skin health by promoting the production of collagen and elastin, which can help to strengthen and protect the skin. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, yellow gentian tincture can provide relief for itchy skin and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yellow gentian tincture helps reduce itchiness by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like glycosides and bitter principles.
Analgesic: The tincture can alleviate itchiness by numbing the affected area, due to the presence of bitter constituents such as amarogentin and sweroside.
Antipruritic: Yellow gentian tincture has an itch-relieving effect, which can be attributed to its active components like secoiridoids and xanthones.
Antimicrobial: The tincture can help prevent skin infections, which can cause itchiness, because of its antimicrobial properties derived from its bitter compounds.
Astringent: Yellow gentian tincture can help dry up excess fluid in the skin, reducing itchiness, due to its astringent properties from tannins and other active constituents.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian plant contain high amounts of iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing properties, making them ideal for treating itchy skin.
Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of the yellow gentian plant are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the yellow gentian plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the yellow gentian plant contain bitter compounds that can help stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation, making them useful for treating itchy skin caused by digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality gentiana lutea roots at dawn, when the dew has evaporated but the plant is still moist.
Clean the harvested roots by gently washing them with cool water and brushing off any debris or dirt.
Chop 200g of cleaned gentiana lutea roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped roots with 400ml of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar, stirring to ensure all plant material is covered.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to facilitate extraction and maturation of the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with itchy skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation. Its active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to alleviate symptoms of skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. Additionally, St. John's wort may help soothe itchy skin by promoting the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that can help regulate mood and reduce itching. Overall, St. John's wort tincture can provide relief for itchy skin by addressing the underlying causes of inflammation and irritation.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant: St. John's wort tincture has antidepressant properties that can help alleviate the psychological discomfort associated with itchy skin, as it increases the levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine.

Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce skin inflammation and irritation, which are important for itchy skin, as it contains active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture has analgesic properties that can help alleviate the pain associated with itchy skin, as it contains active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent skin infections caused by scratching, which are important for itchy skin, as it contains active constituents like hyperforin.
Antipruritic: St. John's wort tincture has antipruritic properties that can help alleviate itching, as it contains active constituents like hyperforin and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy skin.
Leaves: Also contain hypericin and hyperforin, and can help reduce inflammation and ease itching.
Stems: While less potent than the flowers and leaves, stems still contain active compounds that can help with itchy skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain similar compounds to the stems, and can help provide additional relief for itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum plants at peak blooming season, gathering sufficient material for the desired quantity of tincture.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by gently brushing off dirt and debris, then air-drying it for several hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to facilitate extraction, using scissors or a sharp knife with caution to avoid contamination.
Steep 1 part of chopped plant material in 2 parts of high-proof vodka or grain-based spirits for 6-8 weeks, shaking the mixture daily to enhance solubility.
Strain and filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into dark glass bottles, discarding solids and storing the final product at room temperature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy skin with herbal tinctures is by using natural remedies with proven anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Chamomile, calendula, and lavender are excellent choices. Dilute the tinctures in water or add them to your bath. These herbs can effectively alleviate itching, reduce inflammation, and promote skin healing. Always remember to perform a patch test before applying any new topical treatments to ensure you are not sensitive or allergic to the herbs.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal tinctures include eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. These conditions often cause discomfort and inflammation of the skin. Herbal tinctures, such as chamomile, calendula, and witch hazel, can provide relief due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Chamomile, for instance, has been shown to reduce inflammation and provide relief from itching. Calendula, on the other hand, promotes healing and can help reduce redness and irritation. Lastly, witch hazel has astringent properties that can help soothe and heal irritated skin. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
